{
  "1:LEN": {

  "7:LEN": [
    {
      "2:LEN": {
        "1:LEN": {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": " from __future__ import annotations\r\n \r\n import enum\r\n import uuid\r\n from datetime import datetime\r\n from typing import Any, Dict, Optional\r\n \r\n from sqlalchemy import (\r\n     JSON,\r\n     Boolean,\r\n     Column,\r\n     DateTime,\r\n     Enum,\r\n     ForeignKey,\r\n     Integer,\r\n     String,\r\n     Text,\r\n )\r\n from sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase, Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\r\n \r\n \r\n class Base(DeclarativeBase):  # type: ignore[misc]\r\n     pass\r\n \r\n \r\n class JobState(str, enum.Enum):\r\n     QUEUED = \"QUEUED\"\r\n     RUNNING = \"RUNNING\"\r\n     SUCCEEDED = \"SUCCEEDED\"\r\n     FAILED = \"FAILED\"\r\n     CANCELED = \"CANCELED\"\r\n     DEAD = \"DEAD\"\r\n \r\n \r\n class Job(Base):\r\n     __tablename__ = \"jobs\"\r\n \r\n     id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\r\n         String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\r\n     )\r\n     type: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(64), nullable=False)\r\n     queue: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(64), default=\"default\")\r\n \r\n     payload: Mapped[Dict[str, Any]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=False)\r\n \r\n     state: Mapped[JobState] = mapped_column(\r\n         Enum(JobState), default=JobState.QUEUED, nullable=False\r\n     )\r\n \r\n     created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\r\n         DateTime(timezone=True), default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False\r\n     )\r\n     updated_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\r\n         DateTime(timezone=True), default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False\r\n     )\r\n \r\n     idempotency_key: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(128), nullable=True)\r\n \r\n     attempts: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, default=0, nullable=False)\r\n     max_attempts: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, default=3, nullable=False)\r\n \r\n     next_run_at: Mapped[Optional[datetime]] = mapped_column(\r\n         DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\r\n     )\r\n \r\n     schedule: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(128), nullable=True)\r\n \r\n     last_error: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)\r\n \r\n     result: Mapped[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=True)\r\n \r\n     is_enqueued: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False, nullable=False)\r\n     cancel_requested: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False, nullable=False)\r\n \r\n     events: Mapped[list[\"JobEvent\"]] = relationship(\r\n         back_populates=\"job\", cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\"\r\n     )\r\n \r\n \r\n class JobEvent(Base):\r\n     __tablename__ = \"job_events\"\r\n \r\n     id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)\r\n \r\n     job_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\r\n         String(36), ForeignKey(\"jobs.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"), nullable=False\r\n     )\r\n \r\n     ts: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\r\n         DateTime(timezone=True), default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False\r\n     )\r\n \r\n     old_state: Mapped[Optional[JobState]] = mapped_column(Enum(JobState), nullable=True)\r\n     new_state: Mapped[JobState] = mapped_column(Enum(JobState), nullable=False)\r\n \r\n     message: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False)\r\n \r\n     metadata: Mapped[Dict[str, Any]] = mapped_column(JSON, default=dict, nullable=False)\r\n \r\n     job: Mapped[Job] = relationship(back_populates=\"events\")\r\n \r\n \r\n class ScheduleTick(Base):\r\n     __tablename__ = \"schedule_ticks\"\r\n \r\n     id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)\r\n \r\n     schedule_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(128), nullable=False)\r\n     planned_time: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False)\r\n     enqueued_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\r\n         DateTime(timezone=True), default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False\r\n     )\r\n \r\n     __table_args__ = (\r\n         # Composite uniqueness guards against duplicate enqueue for the same logical tick.\r\n         {\"sqlite_autoincrement\": True},\r\n     )\r\n \r\n"
        },
        "2:LEN": "7beb219f28106dc31420c966d255e3697c08c341301d855cc486b07c72b1da97",

        },

        }

    },
    {
      "2:LEN": {
        "1:LEN": {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": " from __future__ import annotations\r\n\r\n from datetime import datetime, timezone\r\n from typing import Any, Dict, Optional\r\n\r\n from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\r\n\r\n from .models import Job, JobEvent, JobState\r\n\r\n\r\n class InvalidStateTransition(Exception):\r\n     pass\r\n\r\n\r\n async def _record_event(\r\n     session: AsyncSession,\r\n     job: Job,\r\n     old: Optional[JobState],\r\n     new: JobState,\r\n     message: str,\r\n     metadata: Dict[str, Any] | None = None,\r\n ) -> None:\r\n     event = JobEvent(\r\n         job_id=job.id,\r\n         old_state=old,\r\n         new_state=new,\r\n         message=message,\r\n         metadata=metadata or {},\r\n     )\r\n     session.add(event)\r\n\r\n\r\n async def transition_state(\r\n     session: AsyncSession,\r\n     job: Job,\r\n     new_state: JobState,\r\n     message: str,\r\n     metadata: Dict[str, Any] | None = None,\r\n ) -> None:\r\n     \"\"\"Validate and perform a state transition, recording a JobEvent.\"\"\"\r\n\r\n     old = job.state\r\n\r\n     if not _is_valid_transition(old, new_state):\r\n         raise InvalidStateTransition(f\"Cannot transition {old} -> {new_state}\")\r\n\r\n     job.state = new_state\r\n     job.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\r\n     await _record_event(session, job, old, new_state, message, metadata)\r\n\r\n\r\n def _is_valid_transition(old: JobState, new: JobState) -> bool:\r\n     if old == new:\r\n         return True\r\n\r\n     if old == JobState.QUEUED and new in {\r\n         JobState.RUNNING,\r\n         JobState.CANCELED,\r\n         JobState.DEAD,\r\n     }:\r\n         return True\r\n\r\n     if old == JobState.RUNNING and new in {\r\n         JobState.SUCCEEDED,\r\n         JobState.FAILED,\r\n         JobState.CANCELED,\r\n         JobState.DEAD,\r\n     }:\r\n         return True\r\n\r\n     if old in {JobState.FAILED, JobState.DEAD} and new == JobState.QUEUED:\r\n         return True\r\n\r\n     # Terminal states remain terminal\r\n     if old in {JobState.SUCCEEDED, JobState.CANCELED}:\r\n         return False\r\n\r\n     return False\r\n\r\n"
        },
        "2:LEN": "181edfe5b7a6fc74cd6312b48b5cfad27e33c7486ddf145b8249809f2b3a2320",
        "3:LEN": "REDACTED"
      },
      "3:LEN": [
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "."
        }
      ],
      "4:VARINT": 1
    },
    {
      "2:LEN": {
        "1:LEN": {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": " from __future__ import annotations\r\n \r\n import asyncio\r\n import logging\r\n import signal\r\n import traceback\r\n from contextlib import asynccontextmanager\r\n from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone\r\n \r\n from prometheus_client import start_http_server\r\n from sqlalchemy import select\r\n from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\r\n \r\n from .backoff import compute_backoff_seconds\r\n from .broker import (\r\n     BrokerMessage,\r\n     ack_job,\r\n     ensure_group,\r\n     enqueue_job,\r\n     push_dead_letter,\r\n     read_jobs,\r\n     reclaim_stuck,\r\n     update_queue_depth,\r\n )\r\n from .config import get_settings\r\n from .db import get_session_factory, init_db\r\n from .logging import configure_logging, get_logger\r\n from .metrics import JOBS_DURATION_SECONDS, JOBS_FINISHED_TOTAL, JOBS_STARTED_TOTAL\r\n from .models import Job, JobState\r\n from .state_machine import InvalidStateTransition, transition_state\r\n from .tasks import get_task_definition, make_context\r\n \r\n \r\n class JobCanceled(Exception):\r\n     pass\r\n \r\n \r\n logger = get_logger(__name__, service=\"worker\")\r\n \r\n \r\n @asynccontextmanager\r\n async def _session_scope() -> AsyncSession:\r\n     factory = get_session_factory()\r\n     async with factory() as session:\r\n         async with session.begin():\r\n             yield session\r\n \r\n \r\n async def _check_canceled(job_id: str) -> None:\r\n     factory = get_session_factory()\r\n     async with factory() as session:\r\n         job = await session.get(Job, job_id)\r\n         if job and job.cancel_requested:\r\n             raise JobCanceled(f\"Job {job_id} canceled\")\r\n \r\n \r\n async def _process_message(msg: BrokerMessage, *, queue: str, consumer_name: str) -> None:\r\n     fields = msg.fields\r\n     job_id = fields.get(\"job_id\")\r\n     if not job_id:\r\n         logger.error(\"Missing job_id in message\", extra={\"stream_id\": msg.entry_id})\r\n         await ack_job(queue, msg.entry_id)\r\n         return\r\n \r\n     settings = get_settings()\r\n \r\n     # Phase 1: transition QUEUED -> RUNNING and increment attempts\r\n     async with _session_scope() as session:\r\n         job = await session.get(Job, job_id)\r\n         if job is None:\r\n             logger.warning(\"Job not found; acking\", extra={\"job_id\": job_id})\r\n             await ack_job(queue, msg.entry_id)\r\n             return\r\n \r\n         if job.state in {JobState.SUCCEEDED, JobState.CANCELED, JobState.DEAD}:\r\n             logger.info(\r\n                 \"Job already in terminal state; acking\",\r\n                 extra={\"job_id\": job_id, \"state\": job.state.value},\r\n             )\r\n             await ack_job(queue, msg.entry_id)\r\n             return\r\n \r\n         # Idempotency: if job has already succeeded, do not re-run side effects.\r\n         if job.idempotency_key and job.state == JobState.SUCCEEDED:\r\n             logger.info(\"Idempotent re-delivery; acking\", extra={\"job_id\": job_id})\r\n             await ack_job(queue, msg.entry_id)\r\n             return\r\n \r\n         # Only process QUEUED jobs here. If state changed unexpectedly, ack.\r\n         if job.state != JobState.QUEUED:\r\n             logger.info(\"Job not QUEUED; skipping\", extra={\"job_id\": job_id, \"state\": job.state.value})\r\n             await ack_job(queue, msg.entry_id)\r\n             return\r\n \r\n         attempt = job.attempts + 1\r\n         job.attempts = attempt\r\n         try:\r\n             await transition_state(session, job, JobState.RUNNING, \"Worker starting execution\")\r\n         except InvalidStateTransition:\r\n             logger.exception(\"Invalid state transition to RUNNING\", extra={\"job_id\": job_id})\r\n             await ack_job(queue, msg.entry_id)\r\n             return\r\n \r\n     # Phase 2: execute task outside transaction\r\n     job_type = fields.get(\"type\") or (job.type if \"job\" in locals() else \"unknown\")  # type: ignore[name-defined]\r\n     task_def = get_task_definition(job_type)\r\n \r\n     import time\r\n \r\n     start = time.monotonic()\r\n     JOBS_STARTED_TOTAL.labels(type=job_type).inc()\r\n \r\n     async def check_canceled() -> None:\r\n         await _check_canceled(job_id)\r\n \r\n     # Reload job state for the context\r\n     factory = get_session_factory()\r\n     async with factory() as exec_session:\r\n         job_for_ctx = await exec_session.get(Job, job_id)\r\n \r\n     ctx_logger = get_logger(f\"miniqueue.task.{task_def.name}\", service=\"worker\")\r\n     ctx = make_context(\r\n         job=job_for_ctx,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\r\n         attempt=attempt,\r\n         session=exec_session,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\r\n         logger=ctx_logger,\r\n         check_canceled=check_canceled,\r\n     )\r\n \r\n     try:\r\n         payload = task_def.payload_model.model_validate(job_for_ctx.payload)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\r\n         result = await task_def.func(ctx, payload)\r\n         exc: BaseException | None = None\r\n         canceled = False\r\n     except JobCanceled:\r\n         result = None\r\n         exc = None\r\n         canceled = True\r\n     except BaseException as e:  # noqa: BLE001\r\n         result = None\r\n         exc = e\r\n         canceled = False\r\n \r\n     duration = time.monotonic() - start\r\n     JOBS_DURATION_SECONDS.labels(type=job_type).observe(duration)\r\n \r\n     # Phase 3: final state transition + result persistence\r\n     async with _session_scope() as session:\r\n         job = await session.get(Job, job_id)\r\n         if job is None:\r\n             logger.error(\"Job disappeared before completion\", extra={\"job_id\": job_id})\r\n             await ack_job(queue, msg.entry_id)\r\n             return\r\n \r\n         if canceled:\r\n             job.result = None\r\n             job.last_error = \"Canceled\"\r\n             job.cancel_requested = False\r\n             await transition_state(session, job, JobState.CANCELED, \"Job canceled cooperatively\")\r\n             JOBS_FINISHED_TOTAL.labels(type=job_type, state=JobState.CANCELED.value).inc()\r\n         elif exc is None:\r\n             job.result = result  # type: ignore[assignment]\r\n             job.last_error = None\r\n             await transition_state(session, job, JobState.SUCCEEDED, \"Job succeeded\")\r\n             JOBS_FINISHED_TOTAL.labels(type=job_type, state=JobState.SUCCEEDED.value).inc()\r\n         else:\r\n             # Failure path: decide between retry and DEAD\r\n             job.last_error = \"\".join(\r\n                 traceback.format_exception_only(type(exc), exc)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\r\n             ).strip()\r\n \r\n             settings = get_settings()\r\n \r\n             if job.attempts >= job.max_attempts:\r\n                 await transition_state(session, job, JobState.DEAD, \"Job reached max attempts\")\r\n                 await push_dead_letter(\r\n                     {\r\n                         \"job_id\": job.id,\r\n                         \"type\": job.type,\r\n                         \"queue\": job.queue,\r\n                         \"error\": job.last_error or \"\",\r\n                     }\r\n                 )\r\n                 JOBS_FINISHED_TOTAL.labels(type=job_type, state=JobState.DEAD.value).inc()\r\n             else:\r\n                 delay = compute_backoff_seconds(\r\n                     job.attempts,\r\n                     base=settings.backoff_base_seconds,\r\n                     multiplier=settings.backoff_multiplier,\r\n                     cap=settings.backoff_cap_seconds,\r\n                 )\r\n                 job.next_run_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(seconds=delay)\r\n                 job.is_enqueued = False\r\n                 await transition_state(session, job, JobState.QUEUED, \"Job scheduled for retry\")\r\n \r\n     # Only XACK after DB commit succeeded.\r\n     await ack_job(queue, msg.entry_id)\r\n \r\n \r\n async def worker_loop(index: int, *, queue: str, consumer_name: str) -> None:\r\n     settings = get_settings()\r\n     while True:\r\n         messages = await read_jobs(\r\n             queue=queue,\r\n             consumer_name=consumer_name,\r\n             count=1,\r\n             block_ms=5_000,\r\n         )\r\n         if not messages:\r\n             continue\r\n         for msg in messages:\r\n             try:\r\n                 await _process_message(msg, queue=queue, consumer_name=consumer_name)\r\n             except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\r\n                 logger.exception(\"Unhandled error in worker loop\", extra={\"consumer\": consumer_name})\r\n \r\n \r\n async def reclaim_loop(*, queue: str, consumer_name: str) -> None:\r\n     settings = get_settings()\r\n     while True:\r\n         await asyncio.sleep(settings.reclaim_interval_sec)\r\n         try:\r\n             await reclaim_stuck(\r\n                 queue=queue,\r\n                 consumer_name=consumer_name,\r\n                 min_idle_ms=settings.visibility_timeout_ms,\r\n             )\r\n             await update_queue_depth(queue)\r\n         except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\r\n             logger.exception(\"Error in reclaim loop\", extra={\"consumer\": consumer_name})\r\n \r\n \r\n async def main() -> None:\r\n     configure_logging(service=\"worker\")\r\n     settings = get_settings()\r\n \r\n     # Start Prometheus metrics server for this process.\r\n     start_http_server(settings.metrics_port)\r\n \r\n     await init_db()\r\n     await ensure_group(settings.queue_default)\r\n \r\n     queue = settings.queue_default\r\n     consumer_name = f\"worker-{id(asyncio.current_task())}\"\r\n \r\n     tasks = [\r\n         asyncio.create_task(worker_loop(i, queue=queue, consumer_name=consumer_name))\r\n         for i in range(settings.worker_concurrency)\r\n     ]\r\n     tasks.append(asyncio.create_task(reclaim_loop(queue=queue, consumer_name=consumer_name)))\r\n \r\n     stop_event = asyncio.Event()\r\n \r\n     loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\r\n \r\n     for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\r\n         loop.add_signal_handler(sig, stop_event.set)\r\n \r\n     await stop_event.wait()\r\n     for t in tasks:\r\n         t.cancel()\r\n     await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)\r\n \r\n \r\n if __name__ == \"__main__\":\r\n     asyncio.run(main())\r\n \r\n"
        },
        "2:LEN": "bdaae6ed00e01b686d6ba1de9fdcd4b56234460c831693f303bcef33733df200",
        "3:LEN": "REDACTED"
      },
      "3:LEN": [
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "."
        }
      ],
      "4:VARINT": 1
    },
    {
      "2:LEN": {
        "1:LEN": {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": " from __future__ import annotations\r\n \r\n import logging\r\n from dataclasses import dataclass\r\n from typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Dict, MutableMapping, Type\r\n \r\n from pydantic import BaseModel\r\n from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\r\n \r\n from ..config import Settings, get_settings\r\n from ..models import Job\r\n \r\n \r\n TaskFunc = Callable[[\"TaskContext\", BaseModel], Awaitable[Any]]\r\n \r\n \r\n @dataclass\r\n class TaskDefinition:\r\n     name: str\r\n     payload_model: Type[BaseModel]\r\n     func: TaskFunc\r\n     max_attempts_default: int\r\n \r\n \r\n @dataclass\r\n class TaskContext:\r\n     job: Job\r\n     attempt: int\r\n     session: AsyncSession\r\n     settings: Settings\r\n     logger: logging.Logger\r\n     check_canceled: Callable[[], Awaitable[None]]\r\n \r\n \r\n _TASKS: MutableMapping[str, TaskDefinition] = {}\r\n \r\n \r\n def register_task(\r\n     name: str,\r\n     *,\r\n     payload_model: Type[BaseModel],\r\n     max_attempts_default: int = 3,\r\n ) -> Callable[[TaskFunc], TaskFunc]:\r\n     \"\"\"Decorator to register a task implementation.\"\"\"\r\n \r\n     def decorator(func: TaskFunc) -> TaskFunc:\r\n         if name in _TASKS:\r\n             raise ValueError(f\"Task {name!r} already registered\")\r\n         _TASKS[name] = TaskDefinition(\r\n             name=name,\r\n             payload_model=payload_model,\r\n             func=func,\r\n             max_attempts_default=max_attempts_default,\r\n         )\r\n         return func\r\n \r\n     return decorator\r\n \r\n \r\n def get_task_definition(name: str) -> TaskDefinition:\r\n     try:\r\n         return _TASKS[name]\r\n     except KeyError as exc:\r\n         raise KeyError(f\"Unknown task type {name!r}\") from exc\r\n \r\n \r\n def make_context(\r\n     *,\r\n     job: Job,\r\n     attempt: int,\r\n     session: AsyncSession,\r\n     logger: logging.Logger,\r\n     check_canceled: Callable[[], Awaitable[None]],\r\n ) -> TaskContext:\r\n     return TaskContext(\r\n         job=job,\r\n         attempt=attempt,\r\n         session=session,\r\n         settings=get_settings(),\r\n         logger=logger,\r\n         check_canceled=check_canceled,\r\n     )\r\n \r\n"
        },
        "2:LEN": "6b88f03489e3eed10b98cf3ab7a6bff2c885ca9540a07658166b79d7ded49e76",
        "3:LEN": "REDACTED"
      },
      "3:LEN": [
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "."
        }
      ],
      "4:VARINT": 1
    },
    {
      "2:LEN": {
        "1:LEN": {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": " from __future__ import annotations\r\n \r\n import asyncio\r\n import time\r\n from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional\r\n \r\n import httpx\r\n from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, HttpUrl\r\n \r\n from . import TaskContext, register_task\r\n \r\n\r\n class SleepPayload(BaseModel):\r\n     seconds: float = Field(..., gt=0)\r\n     step: float = Field(0.5, gt=0)\r\n \r\n\r\n @register_task(\"sleep\", payload_model=SleepPayload)\r\n async def task_sleep(ctx: TaskContext, payload: SleepPayload) -> Dict[str, Any]:\r\n     remaining = payload.seconds\r\n     step = payload.step\r\n     start = time.monotonic()\r\n     while remaining > 0:\r\n         await ctx.check_canceled()\r\n         interval = min(step, remaining)\r\n         await asyncio.sleep(interval)\r\n         remaining -= interval\r\n     return {\"slept\": time.monotonic() - start}\r\n \r\n\r\n class HttpGetPayload(BaseModel):\r\n     url: HttpUrl\r\n     timeout_seconds: float = 5.0\r\n \r\n\r\n @register_task(\"http_get\", payload_model=HttpGetPayload)\r\n async def task_http_get(ctx: TaskContext, payload: HttpGetPayload) -> Dict[str, Any]:\r\n     async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=payload.timeout_seconds) as client:\r\n         resp = await client.get(str(payload.url))\r\n     return {\r\n         \"status_code\": resp.status_code,\r\n         \"headers\": dict(resp.headers),\r\n         \"body\": resp.text[:2048],\r\n     }\r\n \r\n\r\n class CpuBurnPayload(BaseModel):\r\n     iterations: int = Field(..., gt=0, le=5_000_000)\r\n \r\n\r\n @register_task(\"cpu_burn\", payload_model=CpuBurnPayload)\r\n async def task_cpu_burn(ctx: TaskContext, payload: CpuBurnPayload) -> Dict[str, Any]:\r\n     total = 0\r\n     for i in range(payload.iterations):\r\n         total += (i * 3) % 7\r\n         if i % 10_000 == 0:\r\n             await ctx.check_canceled()\r\n     return {\"iterations\": payload.iterations, \"value\": total}\r\n \r\n\r\n class FailThenSucceedPayload(BaseModel):\r\n     fail_attempts: int = Field(..., ge=1)\r\n \r\n\r\n @register_task(\"fail_then_succeed\", payload_model=FailThenSucceedPayload)\r\n async def task_fail_then_succeed(\r\n     ctx: TaskContext, payload: FailThenSucceedPayload\r\n ) -> Dict[str, Any]:\r\n     if ctx.attempt <= payload.fail_attempts:\r\n         raise RuntimeError(f\"Intentional failure on attempt {ctx.attempt}\")\r\n     return {\"succeeded_on_attempt\": ctx.attempt}\r\n \r\n\r\n class PipelineDemoPayload(BaseModel):\r\n     url: HttpUrl\r\n     steps: int = Field(3, ge=1, le=10)\r\n \r\n\r\n @register_task(\"pipeline_demo\", payload_model=PipelineDemoPayload)\r\n async def task_pipeline_demo(\r\n     ctx: TaskContext, payload: PipelineDemoPayload\r\n ) -> Dict[str, Any]:\r\n     ctx.logger.info(\"pipeline_demo: starting\", extra={\"job_id\": ctx.job.id})\r\n \r\n     # Step 1: small sleeps with cancellation checks.\r\n     for i in range(payload.steps):\r\n         await ctx.check_canceled()\r\n         await asyncio.sleep(0.2)\r\n         ctx.logger.info(\"pipeline_demo: sleep step\", extra={\"step\": i})\r\n \r\n     # Step 2: HTTP GET\r\n     await ctx.check_canceled()\r\n     async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5.0) as client:\r\n         resp = await client.get(str(payload.url))\r\n \r\n     # Step 3: CPU-bound transformation\r\n     await ctx.check_canceled()\r\n     body_len = len(resp.text)\r\n     checksum = sum(ord(c) for c in resp.text[:1000])\r\n \r\n     return {\r\n         \"status_code\": resp.status_code,\r\n         \"body_length\": body_len,\r\n         \"checksum\": checksum,\r\n     }\r\n \r\n"
        },
        "2:LEN": "5f9a7e1b4f483bf08f9edd1a09a8144dfa84ef7d80810c4a0b2824eeff048ace",
        "3:LEN": "REDACTED"
      },
      "3:LEN": [
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "."
        }
      ],
      "4:VARINT": 1
    }
  ]
}
```

### RESPONSE ###
```
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true
Access-Control-Expose-Headers: Grpc-Status\, Grpc-Message\, Grpc-Status-Details-Bin\, Content-Encoding\, Connect-Content-Encoding\, traceparent\, backend-traceparent\, x-amzn-trace-id\, x-request-id
Content-Type: application/proto
Date: Thu\, 05 Mar 2026 02:47:53 GMT
Vary: Origin
```

  "1:VARINT": 1
}

### SIGNATURE ###

http:host is localhost
http:method is POST
http:queryparams is -NONE-
http:requestBodyJSON is {"1:LEN": {"1:I64": 4842195278606858022}, "2:LEN": "REDACTED", "6:VARINT": 4, "7:LEN": [{"2:LEN": {"1:LEN": {"1:LEN": "REDACTED", "2:LEN": " from __future__ import annotations\r\n \r\n import enum\r\n import uuid\r\n from datetime import datetime\r\n from typing import Any, Dict, Optional\r\n \r\n from sqlalchemy import (\r\n     JSON,\r\n     Boolean,\r\n     Column,\r\n     DateTime,\r\n     Enum,\r\n     ForeignKey,\r\n     Integer,\r\n     String,\r\n     Text,\r\n )\r\n from sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase, Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\r\n \r\n \r\n class Base(DeclarativeBase):  # type: ignore[misc]\r\n     pass\r\n \r\n \r\n class JobState(str, enum.Enum):\r\n     QUEUED = \"QUEUED\"\r\n     RUNNING = \"RUNNING\"\r\n     SUCCEEDED = \"SUCCEEDED\"\r\n     FAILED = \"FAILED\"\r\n     CANCELED = \"CANCELED\"\r\n     DEAD = \"DEAD\"\r\n \r\n \r\n class Job(Base):\r\n     __tablename__ = \"jobs\"\r\n \r\n     id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\r\n         String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\r\n     )\r\n     type: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(64), nullable=False)\r\n     queue: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(64), default=\"default\")\r\n \r\n     payload: Mapped[Dict[str, Any]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=False)\r\n \r\n     state: Mapped[JobState] = mapped_column(\r\n         Enum(JobState), default=JobState.QUEUED, nullable=False\r\n     )\r\n \r\n     created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\r\n         DateTime(timezone=True), default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False\r\n     )\r\n     updated_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\r\n         DateTime(timezone=True), default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False\r\n     )\r\n \r\n     idempotency_key: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(128), nullable=True)\r\n \r\n     attempts: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, default=0, nullable=False)\r\n     max_attempts: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, default=3, nullable=False)\r\n \r\n     next_run_at: Mapped[Optional[datetime]] = mapped_column(\r\n         DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\r\n     )\r\n \r\n     schedule: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(128), nullable=True)\r\n \r\n     last_error: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)\r\n \r\n     result: Mapped[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=True)\r\n \r\n     is_enqueued: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False, nullable=False)\r\n     cancel_requested: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False, nullable=False)\r\n \r\n     events: Mapped[list[\"JobEvent\"]] = relationship(\r\n         back_populates=\"job\", cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\"\r\n     )\r\n \r\n \r\n class JobEvent(Base):\r\n     __tablename__ = \"job_events\"\r\n \r\n     id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)\r\n \r\n     job_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\r\n         String(36), ForeignKey(\"jobs.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"), nullable=False\r\n     )\r\n \r\n     ts: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\r\n         DateTime(timezone=True), default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False\r\n     )\r\n \r\n     old_state: Mapped[Optional[JobState]] = mapped_column(Enum(JobState), nullable=True)\r\n     new_state: Mapped[JobState] = mapped_column(Enum(JobState), nullable=False)\r\n \r\n     message: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False)\r\n \r\n     metadata: Mapped[Dict[str, Any]] = mapped_column(JSON, default=dict, nullable=False)\r\n \r\n     job: Mapped[Job] = relationship(back_populates=\"events\")\r\n \r\n \r\n class ScheduleTick(Base):\r\n     __tablename__ = \"schedule_ticks\"\r\n \r\n     id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)\r\n \r\n     schedule_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(128), nullable=False)\r\n     planned_time: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False)\r\n     enqueued_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\r\n         DateTime(timezone=True), default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False\r\n     )\r\n \r\n     __table_args__ = (\r\n         # Composite uniqueness guards against duplicate enqueue for the same logical tick.\r\n         {\"sqlite_autoincrement\": True},\r\n     )\r\n \r\n"}, "2:LEN": "7beb219f28106dc31420c966d255e3697c08c341301d855cc486b07c72b1da97", "3:LEN": "REDACTED"}, "3:LEN": [{"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "."}], "4:VARINT": 1}, {"2:LEN": {"1:LEN": {"1:LEN": "REDACTED", "2:LEN": " from __future__ import annotations\r\n\r\n from datetime import datetime, timezone\r\n from typing import Any, Dict, Optional\r\n\r\n from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\r\n\r\n from .models import Job, JobEvent, JobState\r\n\r\n\r\n class InvalidStateTransition(Exception):\r\n     pass\r\n\r\n\r\n async def _record_event(\r\n     session: AsyncSession,\r\n     job: Job,\r\n     old: Optional[JobState],\r\n     new: JobState,\r\n     message: str,\r\n     metadata: Dict[str, Any] | None = None,\r\n ) -> None:\r\n     event = JobEvent(\r\n         job_id=job.id,\r\n         old_state=old,\r\n         new_state=new,\r\n         message=message,\r\n         metadata=metadata or {},\r\n     )\r\n     session.add(event)\r\n\r\n\r\n async def transition_state(\r\n     session: AsyncSession,\r\n     job: Job,\r\n     new_state: JobState,\r\n     message: str,\r\n     metadata: Dict[str, Any] | None = None,\r\n ) -> None:\r\n     \"\"\"Validate and perform a state transition, recording a JobEvent.\"\"\"\r\n\r\n     old = job.state\r\n\r\n     if not _is_valid_transition(old, new_state):\r\n         raise InvalidStateTransition(f\"Cannot transition {old} -> {new_state}\")\r\n\r\n     job.state = new_state\r\n     job.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\r\n     await _record_event(session, job, old, new_state, message, metadata)\r\n\r\n\r\n def _is_valid_transition(old: JobState, new: JobState) -> bool:\r\n     if old == new:\r\n         return True\r\n\r\n     if old == JobState.QUEUED and new in {\r\n         JobState.RUNNING,\r\n         JobState.CANCELED,\r\n         JobState.DEAD,\r\n     }:\r\n         return True\r\n\r\n     if old == JobState.RUNNING and new in {\r\n         JobState.SUCCEEDED,\r\n         JobState.FAILED,\r\n         JobState.CANCELED,\r\n         JobState.DEAD,\r\n     }:\r\n         return True\r\n\r\n     if old in {JobState.FAILED, JobState.DEAD} and new == JobState.QUEUED:\r\n         return True\r\n\r\n     # Terminal states remain terminal\r\n     if old in {JobState.SUCCEEDED, JobState.CANCELED}:\r\n         return False\r\n\r\n     return False\r\n\r\n"}, "2:LEN": "181edfe5b7a6fc74cd6312b48b5cfad27e33c7486ddf145b8249809f2b3a2320", "3:LEN": "REDACTED"}, "3:LEN": [{"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "."}], "4:VARINT": 1}, {"2:LEN": {"1:LEN": {"1:LEN": "REDACTED", "2:LEN": " from __future__ import annotations\r\n \r\n import asyncio\r\n import logging\r\n import signal\r\n import traceback\r\n from contextlib import asynccontextmanager\r\n from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone\r\n \r\n from prometheus_client import start_http_server\r\n from sqlalchemy import select\r\n from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\r\n \r\n from .backoff import compute_backoff_seconds\r\n from .broker import (\r\n     BrokerMessage,\r\n     ack_job,\r\n     ensure_group,\r\n     enqueue_job,\r\n     push_dead_letter,\r\n     read_jobs,\r\n     reclaim_stuck,\r\n     update_queue_depth,\r\n )\r\n from .config import get_settings\r\n from .db import get_session_factory, init_db\r\n from .logging import configure_logging, get_logger\r\n from .metrics import JOBS_DURATION_SECONDS, JOBS_FINISHED_TOTAL, JOBS_STARTED_TOTAL\r\n from .models import Job, JobState\r\n from .state_machine import InvalidStateTransition, transition_state\r\n from .tasks import get_task_definition, make_context\r\n \r\n \r\n class JobCanceled(Exception):\r\n     pass\r\n \r\n \r\n logger = get_logger(__name__, service=\"worker\")\r\n \r\n \r\n @asynccontextmanager\r\n async def _session_scope() -> AsyncSession:\r\n     factory = get_session_factory()\r\n     async with factory() as session:\r\n         async with session.begin():\r\n             yield session\r\n \r\n \r\n async def _check_canceled(job_id: str) -> None:\r\n     factory = get_session_factory()\r\n     async with factory() as session:\r\n         job = await session.get(Job, job_id)\r\n         if job and job.cancel_requested:\r\n             raise JobCanceled(f\"Job {job_id} canceled\")\r\n \r\n \r\n async def _process_message(msg: BrokerMessage, *, queue: str, consumer_name: str) -> None:\r\n     fields = msg.fields\r\n     job_id = fields.get(\"job_id\")\r\n     if not job_id:\r\n         logger.error(\"Missing job_id in message\", extra={\"stream_id\": msg.entry_id})\r\n         await ack_job(queue, msg.entry_id)\r\n         return\r\n \r\n     settings = get_settings()\r\n \r\n     # Phase 1: transition QUEUED -> RUNNING and increment attempts\r\n     async with _session_scope() as session:\r\n         job = await session.get(Job, job_id)\r\n         if job is None:\r\n             logger.warning(\"Job not found; acking\", extra={\"job_id\": job_id})\r\n             await ack_job(queue, msg.entry_id)\r\n             return\r\n \r\n         if job.state in {JobState.SUCCEEDED, JobState.CANCELED, JobState.DEAD}:\r\n             logger.info(\r\n                 \"Job already in terminal state; acking\",\r\n                 extra={\"job_id\": job_id, \"state\": job.state.value},\r\n             )\r\n             await ack_job(queue, msg.entry_id)\r\n             return\r\n \r\n         # Idempotency: if job has already succeeded, do not re-run side effects.\r\n         if job.idempotency_key and job.state == JobState.SUCCEEDED:\r\n             logger.info(\"Idempotent re-delivery; acking\", extra={\"job_id\": job_id})\r\n             await ack_job(queue, msg.entry_id)\r\n             return\r\n \r\n         # Only process QUEUED jobs here. If state changed unexpectedly, ack.\r\n         if job.state != JobState.QUEUED:\r\n             logger.info(\"Job not QUEUED; skipping\", extra={\"job_id\": job_id, \"state\": job.state.value})\r\n             await ack_job(queue, msg.entry_id)\r\n             return\r\n \r\n         attempt = job.attempts + 1\r\n         job.attempts = attempt\r\n         try:\r\n             await transition_state(session, job, JobState.RUNNING, \"Worker starting execution\")\r\n         except InvalidStateTransition:\r\n             logger.exception(\"Invalid state transition to RUNNING\", extra={\"job_id\": job_id})\r\n             await ack_job(queue, msg.entry_id)\r\n             return\r\n \r\n     # Phase 2: execute task outside transaction\r\n     job_type = fields.get(\"type\") or (job.type if \"job\" in locals() else \"unknown\")  # type: ignore[name-defined]\r\n     task_def = get_task_definition(job_type)\r\n \r\n     import time\r\n \r\n     start = time.monotonic()\r\n     JOBS_STARTED_TOTAL.labels(type=job_type).inc()\r\n \r\n     async def check_canceled() -> None:\r\n         await _check_canceled(job_id)\r\n \r\n     # Reload job state for the context\r\n     factory = get_session_factory()\r\n     async with factory() as exec_session:\r\n         job_for_ctx = await exec_session.get(Job, job_id)\r\n \r\n     ctx_logger = get_logger(f\"miniqueue.task.{task_def.name}\", service=\"worker\")\r\n     ctx = make_context(\r\n         job=job_for_ctx,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\r\n         attempt=attempt,\r\n         session=exec_session,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\r\n         logger=ctx_logger,\r\n         check_canceled=check_canceled,\r\n     )\r\n \r\n     try:\r\n         payload = task_def.payload_model.model_validate(job_for_ctx.payload)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\r\n         result = await task_def.func(ctx, payload)\r\n         exc: BaseException | None = None\r\n         canceled = False\r\n     except JobCanceled:\r\n         result = None\r\n         exc = None\r\n         canceled = True\r\n     except BaseException as e:  # noqa: BLE001\r\n         result = None\r\n         exc = e\r\n         canceled = False\r\n \r\n     duration = time.monotonic() - start\r\n     JOBS_DURATION_SECONDS.labels(type=job_type).observe(duration)\r\n \r\n     # Phase 3: final state transition + result persistence\r\n     async with _session_scope() as session:\r\n         job = await session.get(Job, job_id)\r\n         if job is None:\r\n             logger.error(\"Job disappeared before completion\", extra={\"job_id\": job_id})\r\n             await ack_job(queue, msg.entry_id)\r\n             return\r\n \r\n         if canceled:\r\n             job.result = None\r\n             job.last_error = \"Canceled\"\r\n             job.cancel_requested = False\r\n             await transition_state(session, job, JobState.CANCELED, \"Job canceled cooperatively\")\r\n             JOBS_FINISHED_TOTAL.labels(type=job_type, state=JobState.CANCELED.value).inc()\r\n         elif exc is None:\r\n             job.result = result  # type: ignore[assignment]\r\n             job.last_error = None\r\n             await transition_state(session, job, JobState.SUCCEEDED, \"Job succeeded\")\r\n             JOBS_FINISHED_TOTAL.labels(type=job_type, state=JobState.SUCCEEDED.value).inc()\r\n         else:\r\n             # Failure path: decide between retry and DEAD\r\n             job.last_error = \"\".join(\r\n                 traceback.format_exception_only(type(exc), exc)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\r\n             ).strip()\r\n \r\n             settings = get_settings()\r\n \r\n             if job.attempts >= job.max_attempts:\r\n                 await transition_state(session, job, JobState.DEAD, \"Job reached max attempts\")\r\n                 await push_dead_letter(\r\n                     {\r\n                         \"job_id\": job.id,\r\n                         \"type\": job.type,\r\n                         \"queue\": job.queue,\r\n                         \"error\": job.last_error or \"\",\r\n                     }\r\n                 )\r\n                 JOBS_FINISHED_TOTAL.labels(type=job_type, state=JobState.DEAD.value).inc()\r\n             else:\r\n                 delay = compute_backoff_seconds(\r\n                     job.attempts,\r\n                     base=settings.backoff_base_seconds,\r\n                     multiplier=settings.backoff_multiplier,\r\n                     cap=settings.backoff_cap_seconds,\r\n                 )\r\n                 job.next_run_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(seconds=delay)\r\n                 job.is_enqueued = False\r\n                 await transition_state(session, job, JobState.QUEUED, \"Job scheduled for retry\")\r\n \r\n     # Only XACK after DB commit succeeded.\r\n     await ack_job(queue, msg.entry_id)\r\n \r\n \r\n async def worker_loop(index: int, *, queue: str, consumer_name: str) -> None:\r\n     settings = get_settings()\r\n     while True:\r\n         messages = await read_jobs(\r\n             queue=queue,\r\n             consumer_name=consumer_name,\r\n             count=1,\r\n             block_ms=5_000,\r\n         )\r\n         if not messages:\r\n             continue\r\n         for msg in messages:\r\n             try:\r\n                 await _process_message(msg, queue=queue, consumer_name=consumer_name)\r\n             except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\r\n                 logger.exception(\"Unhandled error in worker loop\", extra={\"consumer\": consumer_name})\r\n \r\n \r\n async def reclaim_loop(*, queue: str, consumer_name: str) -> None:\r\n     settings = get_settings()\r\n     while True:\r\n         await asyncio.sleep(settings.reclaim_interval_sec)\r\n         try:\r\n             await reclaim_stuck(\r\n                 queue=queue,\r\n                 consumer_name=consumer_name,\r\n                 min_idle_ms=settings.visibility_timeout_ms,\r\n             )\r\n             await update_queue_depth(queue)\r\n         except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\r\n             logger.exception(\"Error in reclaim loop\", extra={\"consumer\": consumer_name})\r\n \r\n \r\n async def main() -> None:\r\n     configure_logging(service=\"worker\")\r\n     settings = get_settings()\r\n \r\n     # Start Prometheus metrics server for this process.\r\n     start_http_server(settings.metrics_port)\r\n \r\n     await init_db()\r\n     await ensure_group(settings.queue_default)\r\n \r\n     queue = settings.queue_default\r\n     consumer_name = f\"worker-{id(asyncio.current_task())}\"\r\n \r\n     tasks = [\r\n         asyncio.create_task(worker_loop(i, queue=queue, consumer_name=consumer_name))\r\n         for i in range(settings.worker_concurrency)\r\n     ]\r\n     tasks.append(asyncio.create_task(reclaim_loop(queue=queue, consumer_name=consumer_name)))\r\n \r\n     stop_event = asyncio.Event()\r\n \r\n     loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\r\n \r\n     for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\r\n         loop.add_signal_handler(sig, stop_event.set)\r\n \r\n     await stop_event.wait()\r\n     for t in tasks:\r\n         t.cancel()\r\n     await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)\r\n \r\n \r\n if __name__ == \"__main__\":\r\n     asyncio.run(main())\r\n \r\n"}, "2:LEN": "bdaae6ed00e01b686d6ba1de9fdcd4b56234460c831693f303bcef33733df200", "3:LEN": "REDACTED"}, "3:LEN": [{"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "."}], "4:VARINT": 1}, {"2:LEN": {"1:LEN": {"1:LEN": "REDACTED", "2:LEN": " from __future__ import annotations\r\n \r\n import logging\r\n from dataclasses import dataclass\r\n from typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Dict, MutableMapping, Type\r\n \r\n from pydantic import BaseModel\r\n from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\r\n \r\n from ..config import Settings, get_settings\r\n from ..models import Job\r\n \r\n \r\n TaskFunc = Callable[[\"TaskContext\", BaseModel], Awaitable[Any]]\r\n \r\n \r\n @dataclass\r\n class TaskDefinition:\r\n     name: str\r\n     payload_model: Type[BaseModel]\r\n     func: TaskFunc\r\n     max_attempts_default: int\r\n \r\n \r\n @dataclass\r\n class TaskContext:\r\n     job: Job\r\n     attempt: int\r\n     session: AsyncSession\r\n     settings: Settings\r\n     logger: logging.Logger\r\n     check_canceled: Callable[[], Awaitable[None]]\r\n \r\n \r\n _TASKS: MutableMapping[str, TaskDefinition] = {}\r\n \r\n \r\n def register_task(\r\n     name: str,\r\n     *,\r\n     payload_model: Type[BaseModel],\r\n     max_attempts_default: int = 3,\r\n ) -> Callable[[TaskFunc], TaskFunc]:\r\n     \"\"\"Decorator to register a task implementation.\"\"\"\r\n \r\n     def decorator(func: TaskFunc) -> TaskFunc:\r\n         if name in _TASKS:\r\n             raise ValueError(f\"Task {name!r} already registered\")\r\n         _TASKS[name] = TaskDefinition(\r\n             name=name,\r\n             payload_model=payload_model,\r\n             func=func,\r\n             max_attempts_default=max_attempts_default,\r\n         )\r\n         return func\r\n \r\n     return decorator\r\n \r\n \r\n def get_task_definition(name: str) -> TaskDefinition:\r\n     try:\r\n         return _TASKS[name]\r\n     except KeyError as exc:\r\n         raise KeyError(f\"Unknown task type {name!r}\") from exc\r\n \r\n \r\n def make_context(\r\n     *,\r\n     job: Job,\r\n     attempt: int,\r\n     session: AsyncSession,\r\n     logger: logging.Logger,\r\n     check_canceled: Callable[[], Awaitable[None]],\r\n ) -> TaskContext:\r\n     return TaskContext(\r\n         job=job,\r\n         attempt=attempt,\r\n         session=session,\r\n         settings=get_settings(),\r\n         logger=logger,\r\n         check_canceled=check_canceled,\r\n     )\r\n \r\n"}, "2:LEN": "6b88f03489e3eed10b98cf3ab7a6bff2c885ca9540a07658166b79d7ded49e76", "3:LEN": "REDACTED"}, "3:LEN": [{"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "."}], "4:VARINT": 1}, {"2:LEN": {"1:LEN": {"1:LEN": "REDACTED", "2:LEN": " from __future__ import annotations\r\n \r\n import asyncio\r\n import time\r\n from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional\r\n \r\n import httpx\r\n from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, HttpUrl\r\n \r\n from . import TaskContext, register_task\r\n \r\n\r\n class SleepPayload(BaseModel):\r\n     seconds: float = Field(..., gt=0)\r\n     step: float = Field(0.5, gt=0)\r\n \r\n\r\n @register_task(\"sleep\", payload_model=SleepPayload)\r\n async def task_sleep(ctx: TaskContext, payload: SleepPayload) -> Dict[str, Any]:\r\n     remaining = payload.seconds\r\n     step = payload.step\r\n     start = time.monotonic()\r\n     while remaining > 0:\r\n         await ctx.check_canceled()\r\n         interval = min(step, remaining)\r\n         await asyncio.sleep(interval)\r\n         remaining -= interval\r\n     return {\"slept\": time.monotonic() - start}\r\n \r\n\r\n class HttpGetPayload(BaseModel):\r\n     url: HttpUrl\r\n     timeout_seconds: float = 5.0\r\n \r\n\r\n @register_task(\"http_get\", payload_model=HttpGetPayload)\r\n async def task_http_get(ctx: TaskContext, payload: HttpGetPayload) -> Dict[str, Any]:\r\n     async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=payload.timeout_seconds) as client:\r\n         resp = await client.get(str(payload.url))\r\n     return {\r\n         \"status_code\": resp.status_code,\r\n         \"headers\": dict(resp.headers),\r\n         \"body\": resp.text[:2048],\r\n     }\r\n \r\n\r\n class CpuBurnPayload(BaseModel):\r\n     iterations: int = Field(..., gt=0, le=5_000_000)\r\n \r\n\r\n @register_task(\"cpu_burn\", payload_model=CpuBurnPayload)\r\n async def task_cpu_burn(ctx: TaskContext, payload: CpuBurnPayload) -> Dict[str, Any]:\r\n     total = 0\r\n     for i in range(payload.iterations):\r\n         total += (i * 3) % 7\r\n         if i % 10_000 == 0:\r\n             await ctx.check_canceled()\r\n     return {\"iterations\": payload.iterations, \"value\": total}\r\n \r\n\r\n class FailThenSucceedPayload(BaseModel):\r\n     fail_attempts: int = Field(..., ge=1)\r\n \r\n\r\n @register_task(\"fail_then_succeed\", payload_model=FailThenSucceedPayload)\r\n async def task_fail_then_succeed(\r\n     ctx: TaskContext, payload: FailThenSucceedPayload\r\n ) -> Dict[str, Any]:\r\n     if ctx.attempt <= payload.fail_attempts:\r\n         raise RuntimeError(f\"Intentional failure on attempt {ctx.attempt}\")\r\n     return {\"succeeded_on_attempt\": ctx.attempt}\r\n \r\n\r\n class PipelineDemoPayload(BaseModel):\r\n     url: HttpUrl\r\n     steps: int = Field(3, ge=1, le=10)\r\n \r\n\r\n @register_task(\"pipeline_demo\", payload_model=PipelineDemoPayload)\r\n async def task_pipeline_demo(\r\n     ctx: TaskContext, payload: PipelineDemoPayload\r\n ) -> Dict[str, Any]:\r\n     ctx.logger.info(\"pipeline_demo: starting\", extra={\"job_id\": ctx.job.id})\r\n \r\n     # Step 1: small sleeps with cancellation checks.\r\n     for i in range(payload.steps):\r\n         await ctx.check_canceled()\r\n         await asyncio.sleep(0.2)\r\n         ctx.logger.info(\"pipeline_demo: sleep step\", extra={\"step\": i})\r\n \r\n     # Step 2: HTTP GET\r\n     await ctx.check_canceled()\r\n     async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5.0) as client:\r\n         resp = await client.get(str(payload.url))\r\n \r\n     # Step 3: CPU-bound transformation\r\n     await ctx.check_canceled()\r\n     body_len = len(resp.text)\r\n     checksum = sum(ord(c) for c in resp.text[:1000])\r\n \r\n     return {\r\n         \"status_code\": resp.status_code,\r\n         \"body_length\": body_len,\r\n         \"checksum\": checksum,\r\n     }\r\n \r\n"}, "2:LEN": "5f9a7e1b4f483bf08f9edd1a09a8144dfa84ef7d80810c4a0b2824eeff048ace", "3:LEN": "REDACTED"}, "3:LEN": [{"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "."}], "4:VARINT": 1}]}
http:url is /aiserver.v1.RepositoryService/FastUpdateFileV2
```
